claims.claim: InterruptionMetrics defined with num_interruptions, num_backchannels, detection_delay, prediction_duration at metrics/base.py:166-181

claims.claim: VAQI definitions: I=agent speaks before user finishes; M=fails to respond within window after turn ends; L=UserStoppedSpeaking to AgentStartedSpeaking
claims.found: Interruptions (I): how often the agent speaks before the user finishes; Missed responses (M): ...within a defined window after a turn ends; Latency (L): elapsed time from UserStoppedSpeaking to AgentStartedSpeaking

claims.claim: Quote: derived directly from event timestamps, well suited for automated testing
claims.found: These metrics are derived directly from event timestamps, making them well suited for automated testing (lines 972-973)

claims.claim: Telephony scorecard: end-of-turn first-audio latency, barge-in success rate, clipped/interrupted frequency, call-abandonment-during-silence
claims.found: End-of-turn to first audio response latency; Barge-in success rate; Frequency of clipped or interrupted responses; Call abandonment during silence

claims.claim: VAQI improvements correlate strongly with perceived experience; measurable gains after tuning end-of-turn thresholds or optimizing orchestration latency
claims.found: Improvements in VAQI correlate strongly with perceived experience. ...teams often see measurable gains after tuning end-of-turn thresholds or optimizing orchestration latency

claims.claim: M (silence/stall) maps to Deepgram signal: Silence or stall detection, where expected responses fail to arrive within defined time windows
claims.found: Silence or stall detection, where expected responses fail to arrive within defined time windows (lines 1047-1048)

claims.claim: Deepgram pairs VAQI with Coval for large-scale probabilistic scoring
claims.found: For large-scale simulation and behavioral evaluation, teams often pair this telemetry with tools from Coval, which specializes in probabilistic testing and multi-turn agent evaluation

claims.claim: num_backchannels exists so backchannels aren't miscounted as failed interruptions
claims.found: num_backchannels:177 and num_interruptions:175 are separate counters; field comment 'Number of backchannels detected, incrementally counted' confirms distinct tracking

claims.claim: InterruptionMetrics is a raw counter source for I; LiveKit ships constituents but no rolled-up index
claims.found: Class carries raw int/float counters (num_interruptions, num_backchannels, detection_delay) with no composite/index field; consistent with note's claim

claims.claim: Qualitative evaluation assesses meaning/intent/task success (framing of two complementary dimensions)
claims.found: Quantitative evaluation measures timing, flow, and responsiveness at the event level. Qualitative evaluation assesses meaning, intent alignment, and task success

summary: All code_refs and source citations verified against ground truth. Both LiveKit code_refs land exactly on cited lines. All deepgram-ebook line citations match the parsed PDF, including the verbatim quotes. Mechanism claims (backchannel split, raw-counter-vs-index distinction) are accurate.
